observation_summary: The compiler fails with a RuntimeException when a --define flag is provided with a string value enclosed in double quotes, and certain constant expressions are not being removed as expected.
hypothesis: The logic for parsing --define flags in AbstractCommandLineRunner was missing a check for double-quoted strings, and the logic for identifying side-effect-free expressions in RemoveConstantExpressions was too restrictive, failing to account for CALL and NEW nodes.
prediction: The code will be updated to include a check for double-quoted strings in the flag parsing logic and to include CALL and NEW nodes in the side-effect check.
experiment_rationale: The fix diff shows an added condition (|| (defValue.charAt(0) == '"' && defValue.charAt(defValue.length() - 1) == '"')) in the flag parser and additional checks (|| exprBody.getType() == Token.NEW || exprBody.getType() == Token.CALL) in the expression removal logic, confirming that the issue was missing validation/condition logic.
reasoning_summary: The bug is primarily a 'Checking' issue because the fix involves adding missing conditional logic (guards) to handle double-quoted strings and to correctly identify nodes that should be treated as side-effect-free. While the second part of the fix (in RemoveConstantExpressions) could be seen as algorithmic, it is fundamentally a predicate check on node types to determine if an expression can be removed, which fits the 'Checking' definition of correcting predicate logic.

alternative_types.why_not_primary: While the fix modifies logic, the core issue is the absence of necessary checks (guards) for specific input formats and node types, which is the hallmark of a Checking defect.
